join_order.notes: sub_valid bitset[86135] = 10.5 KB L1-resident; sub_sic_arr and sub_cik_arr flat arrays for O(1) lookup

Parallel pre scan: 64 thread-local mini flat hash sets (2048 entries x 8B = 16KB each, L1-resident); union into global pre_eq_sub_set after parallel scan. pre_eq_sub_set: 131072 slots x 8B = 1 MB L2-resident. O(1) probe replaces 154 MB pre_key_sorted binary search.

join_order.probe: result groups only (deferred to output decode)
join_order.notes: Defer tag join to output stage. Only few K unique (tag_c, ver_c) pairs in result groups. FNV computed from raw dict bytes (no std::string). tag_pk_hash probed once per unique pair.

filters.method: zone map block skip + per-row uom_code check
pre_eq_sub_set flat hash probe; O(1); 1 MB L2-resident

data_structures.dict_loading: raw mmap + uint32_t offset array (no std::string). For each dict file [n:uint32][len:uint16, bytes...]*n: parse to build offsets[] array pointing into raw bytes. Access entry i as (raw_ptr + offsets[i], offsets[i+1] - offsets[i]). Eliminates ~282K std::string allocations (~14ms savings). Tag dict: 198311 entries, ver dict: 83815 entries.
data_structures.sub_valid_bitset: uint64_t[(86135+63)/64] = 10.5 KB L1-resident; marks adsh_codes where sic in [4000,4999]
data_structures.sub_sic_array: int16_t[86135] = 168 KB; direct lookup sic value by adsh_code
data_structures.sub_cik_array: int32_t[86135] = 336 KB; direct lookup cik by adsh_code
data_structures.thread_local_pre_mini_set: per-thread flat OA hash set; capacity=2048 slots x 8B = 16KB; L1-resident. Same key packing as pre_eq_sub_set: adsh_c<<36|tag_c<<18|ver_c. After parallel pre scan, union all thread-local sets into global pre_eq_sub_set (131072 slots).
data_structures.pre_eq_sub_set: flat open-addressing hash set; key=uint64_t packed (adsh_c<<36|tag_c<<18|ver_c); capacity=131072; 131072*8=1 MB; L2-resident; empty=UINT64_MAX
data_structures.thread_local_agg: per-thread CUSTOM flat open-addressing hash map (NOT std::unordered_map). Layout: two parallel arrays — uint64_t keys[16384] (128KB) + AggAccum vals[16384] where AggAccum={double sum_val; int64_t cnt} (196KB). Total 324KB per thread, fits L2. Hash: multiply-shift on key, linear probe. Empty sentinel: key=UINT64_MAX. Aggregate key = uint64_t packed (uint16_t(sic)<<48 | uint32_t(tag_c)<<18 | uint32_t(ver_c)). NO std::unordered_map anywhere.
data_structures.thread_local_packed_cik: per-thread vector<uint64_t> of 62-bit packed CIK keys. Per qualifying num row: packed = ((uint64_t)(sic-4000) << 52) | ((uint64_t)(uint32_t)tag_c << 34) | ((uint64_t)(uint32_t)ver_c << 17) | (uint64_t)(uint32_t)adsh_c. Bits: sic_offset[10b|52-61] tag_c[18b|34-51] ver_c[17b|17-33] adsh_c[17b|0-16]. 62 bits fit uint64_t. After scan, per-thread sort+unique reduces per-thread vector to unique (group,adsh) pairs; expected ~1K-2K entries per thread after dedup.
data_structures.global_packed_cik: After per-thread dedup: collect into single vector<uint64_t>, std::sort, then linear scan for final dedup. Total unique pairs bounded by ~4048 adsh_codes x distinct groups = ~50K-100K entries (8B each = 400-800KB). This replaces the previous 530K x 16B CIK sort (8.5MB) and eliminates the second fg_cik re-sort in resolve_tlabels.
data_structures.global_flat_agg: flat open-addressing hash map (same layout as thread-local but capacity=32768). After parallel scan, merge by iterating each thread-local flat map sequentially (sequential scan of 16384-slot arrays = cache-friendly). Insert into global flat map.

parallelism.pre_scan: PARALLEL morsel-driven over pre blocks (96 blocks of 100K rows). Each thread uses a thread-local mini flat hash set (2048 slots, L1-resident) for its pre rows. After scan: each thread flushes its mini set into global pre_eq_sub_set using a single-writer critical section (or atomic CAS). Since only ~64K unique keys exist and each thread contributes ~1K entries, flush is fast.
parallelism.num_scan: morsel-driven over num blocks (395 blocks); atomic block counter; each thread processes complete blocks
parallelism.merge_phase: Flat map merge: iterate each thread's flat key array sequentially (16384 entries, most empty), probe global flat map for non-empty slots. Sequential access pattern, cache-friendly. CIK merge: sort per-thread packed vectors (8K entries/thread, trivial), collect all, global sort+dedup, linear scan for distinct cik counting per group.

indexes.purpose: block-level USD filter: skip blocks where uom_min > 0
output decode only: for each unique (tag_c, ver_c) in result groups, compute FNV64 hash using raw dict bytes (no std::string), probe tag_pk_hash to get tag row_id, check abstract[row_id], read tlabel

aggregation.key: uint64_t packed(uint16_t(sic)<<48 | uint32_t(tag_c)<<18 | uint32_t(ver_c)) — 8 bytes; tag_c < 198312 < 2^18; ver_c < 83816 < 2^17
aggregation.implementation: custom flat open-addressing map (NOT std::unordered_map); capacity 16384 per thread, 32768 global; linear probe on multiply-shift hash; empty sentinel UINT64_MAX
aggregation.accumulator: double sum_val, int64_t cnt (for AVG = sum/cnt); COUNT DISTINCT cik via sorted packed_cik dedup (no per-group CIK sets in hot path)
aggregation.cik_distinct_count: after global sort+dedup of packed_cik keys: linear scan, decode (sic-4000, tag_c, ver_c, adsh_c) from each key, look up cik=sub_cik[adsh_c], count distinct cik per (sic_offset, tag_c, ver_c) group directly; store in flat array indexed by group position in global_flat_agg
aggregation.post_scan_tlabel_merge: after resolve_tlabels phase, groups with same (sic, tlabel_string) merged into final result

columns: t.tlabel (decoded via tag_pk_hash at output stage using raw dict bytes for FNV)

optimization_notes: Root causes: (1) merge_aggregates (60ms) dominated by STL unordered_map chained-bucket overhead across 64 thread-local maps and by sorting 530K 16-byte CIK entries with many duplicates (Category B + Category A); (2) resolve_tlabels (24ms) had a redundant second sort of the same ~530K-entry fg_cik vector (Category A); (3) data_loading (26ms) allocated ~282K std::string objects for dict entries that are only accessed for a few thousand FNV hash lookups (Category C); (4) build_pre_eq_sub_set (11ms) was single-threaded on 9.6M rows despite 64 cores available (Category D). Fixes: replace all std::unordered_map with custom flat open-addressing hash maps (two parallel arrays: uint64_t keys + AggAccum vals, capacity 16384 per thread); replace per-row (group_key:8B, cik:4B, pad:4B) CIK entries with 62-bit packed keys encoding (sic-4000:10b | tag_c:18b | ver_c:17b | adsh_c:17b) — sort+dedup per-thread first (8K entries, trivial), then global sort of ~50-100K unique (group,adsh) pairs instead of 530K duplicated 16-byte structs, decode adsh_c→cik at count stage; this eliminates the second sort in resolve_tlabels entirely; load dicts as raw mmap + uint32_t offset arrays (zero std::string allocations), compute FNV directly on raw byte ranges; parallelize pre scan with 64 thread-local mini flat sets (2048x8B=16KB, L1-resident), unioned into global set. Projected savings: merge_aggregates 60→15ms, resolve_tlabels 24→6ms, data_loading 26→12ms, build_pre_eq 11→2ms, total ~120ms→~60ms hot.
